column1: DOP-C02,"Domain 4: Monitoring & Logging","Monitoring and Logging","## DOP-C02 Domain 4: Monitoring and Logging
This domain covers log/metric collection, auditing, analysis, and automated event management. Expect cross-account aggregation, metric math, distributed tracing, and automated remediation.
**What EC2 Basic Monitoring does NOT include:** Memory, disk space, processes, swap (requires CloudWatch Agent).
- Collects OS metrics (memory, disk, swap, processes)
- Configuration stored in SSM Parameter Store
EMF: Write JSON with `_aws` metadata -> CloudWatch auto-extracts metrics. No API calls needed.

column4: Alert only when multiple signals confirm

column1: - **DatapointsToAlarm:** M out of N must breach (reduces noise)
ALARM(HighCPU) AND ALARM(HighErrorRate)  -> Only page when both confirm
ALARM(AppDown) AND NOT ALARM(Maintenance) -> Suppress during maintenance
Reduces alert noise. Only alert when multiple signals confirm an issue.
Common expressions: error rate %, rate per second, SUM/AVG across dimensions, ANOMALY_DETECTION_BAND, FILL for missing data.

column1: - **""Alert on error rate > 5%""** -> Metric Math alarm
- **""Centralize logs from multiple accounts""** -> Cross-Account Observability or Subscription Filters
- **""Long-term archive cheaply""** -> Export to S3 or Firehose -> S3
- **""Reduce log costs""** -> Infrequent Access log class
- **""Detect root login""** -> CloudTrail -> Logs -> Metric Filter -> Alarm
- **""Identify bottleneck in microservices""** -> X-Ray Service Map
- **""Search traces by customer ID""** -> X-Ray Annotations
- **""Prove logs not tampered""** -> CloudTrail log file validation
- **""Query audit events with SQL""** -> CloudTrail Lake
- **CloudWatch Agent** = memory + disk metrics. Basic monitoring doesn't include them.
- **EMF** creates metrics from structured logs automatically. No PutMetricData needed.
- **Metric Filters** extract metrics from unstructured logs. Pattern -> Metric -> Alarm.
- **Composite Alarms** reduce noise. AND logic: only alert when multiple signals confirm.
- **Anomaly Detection** uses ML. No fixed thresholds needed. Adapts to patterns.
- **Logs Insights** = ad-hoc log analysis. Know syntax: fields, filter, stats, sort, parse.
- **Subscription Filters** stream logs real-time. Max 2 per log group.
- **CloudTrail + CloudWatch Logs** = real-time security alerting pattern.
- **CloudTrail Insights** = unusual API activity (spikes, error rates).
- **X-Ray Annotations** = indexed, searchable. Metadata = not searchable.
- **X-Ray Sampling** controls cost. Custom rules: 100% errors, 5% normal.
- **EventBridge** = central nervous system for automation. 200+ AWS service sources.
- **Cross-Account Observability (OAM)** = view metrics/logs/traces across accounts without moving data.
- **Synthetics canaries** = proactive monitoring. Test endpoints on schedule.
- **Contributor Insights** = top-N analysis (top talkers, heaviest users).
- **Incident Manager** = structured response with escalation and runbooks.
- **VPC Flow Logs** = network metadata (not payload). Use Traffic Mirroring for packets.
- **ADOT** = vendor-neutral OpenTelemetry. Single instrumentation, multiple backends.
- **Managed Grafana** = unified dashboards across CloudWatch, X-Ray, Prometheus.
This covers all objectives in DOP-C02 Domain 4:
- **CloudWatch Metrics** - Agent, custom metrics, EMF, resolution, retention
- **CloudWatch Alarms** - Metric, composite, anomaly detection, metric math
- **CloudWatch Logs** - Groups, streams, metric filters, Insights, subscriptions
- **CloudTrail** - Management/data events, Insights, Lake, security monitoring
- **X-Ray** - Traces, segments, annotations, sampling, service map
- **Centralized Observability** - OAM, ADOT, Managed Grafana, Managed Prometheus
- **Incident Management** - Response plans, escalation, runbooks, post-incident
- **Cross-Account Observability (OAM)** is the modern approach. Monitoring account views metrics/logs/traces from source accounts without moving data. Set up via Organizations for automatic linking.
- **Synthetics canaries** proactively test endpoints before users report issues. Heartbeat = availability; GUI workflow = multi-step flows. Failed canaries trigger alarms automatically.
- **Contributor Insights** answers ""who is causing this?"" Top IPs by error count, heaviest API consumers. Real-time top-N rankings.
- **VPC Flow Logs** = network metadata (not content). S3 + Athena for large volumes (cheapest). CloudWatch Logs for real-time metric filters.
- **Incident Manager** provides structured response. Response plans + escalation + runbooks + chat channels. Auto-escalate if no acknowledgment.
- **Managed Grafana** = ""unified dashboard across multiple data sources."" Connects to CloudWatch, Prometheus, X-Ray, Timestream, third-party.
- **ADOT** = vendor-neutral OpenTelemetry. Single instrumentation, multiple backends. Preferred for containers and multi-cloud.
- **Log retention strategy:** Active (CloudWatch, 30-90 days) -> Archive (S3 Standard, 1 year) -> Compliance (S3 Glacier, 7+ years).
- **TreatMissingData:** For scaling alarms use ""missing"" (maintain state). For health alarms use ""breaching"" (assume worst).
- **EventBridge Scheduler vs. Rules:** Scheduler = millions of schedules, one-time, timezone-aware. Rules = event-pattern matching.

column2: +-- Custom Metrics: PutMetricData or EMF (1-sec to 5-min)
+-- Embedded Metric Format: Metrics from structured logs
+-- Metric Math: Calculated metrics (error rate, ratios)
+-- Metric Alarms: Single threshold (M out of N)
+-- Composite Alarms: AND/OR/NOT across multiple alarms
+-- Anomaly Detection: ML-based band; no fixed threshold
+-- Actions: SNS, Auto Scaling, EC2, Lambda
+-- Collection: Agent, awslogs driver, SDK, service integration
+-- Metric Filters: Extract metrics from log patterns
+-- Cross-Account: OAM or subscriptions to central account
+-- Retention: 1 day to never; export to S3 for archive
+-- Log Classes: Standard vs. Infrequent Access (50% cheaper)
+-- Contributor Insights: Top-N analysis from logs
+-- Management Events: API calls managing resources (default ON)
+-- Insights Events: Unusual API activity detection
+-- Organization Trail: All accounts, all regions
+-- Log File Validation: SHA-256 digest (tamper detection)
+-- CloudTrail Lake: SQL queries (up to 7 years)
+-- Service Map: Visual architecture with latency/errors
+-- Targets: Lambda, Step Functions, SQS, SNS, SSM, API Destinations
+-- Archive & Replay: Store events; replay for debugging
+-- Incident Manager: Response plans, escalation, runbooks
+-- Flow: Alarm -> Incident -> Engage -> Remediate -> Review
